problem_summary: 2014 MCM Problem C (ICM, interdisciplinary): build network science models to measure the influence/importance of "players" (nodes) in different networks. The team had to (a) construct the Erdős co-authorship network from supplied data, (b) define better influence measures than off-the-shelf centrality, (c) apply them to a second, self-collected network, and (d) handle the directed paper-citation network of the 16 papers supplied in the ICM problem file. Core deliverable: a defensible influence-ranking methodology that respects edge weight, structure, and node order, plus extension to directed graphs.
models_used: Shapley-value influence measure for UNDIRECTED networks: combines cooperative game theory (Shapley value, Shapley 1953) with a network-cohesion measure to score each node by its marginal contribution to overall cohesion
Borgatti (2006) KPP-POS / KPP-NEG distance-based cohesion measure DF = 1 - [2*sum_{i>j}(1/d_ij)] / [n(n-1)], used as the characteristic/value function v in the Shapley computation

Custom weighting of the cohesion distance: d_ij = 1 + 1/p_ij for linked nodes (p_ij = edge weight), infinite otherwise — this is the team's own modification to make Borgatti's measure handle WEIGHTED networks

Monte Carlo / random-permutation sampling approximation of the Shapley value (average marginal contribution over k randomly sampled permutations instead of all N! permutations), justified as an unbiased estimator
Basic PageRank (Brin-Page) for DIRECTED citation network: PR(p_i) = d * sum PR(p_j)/L(p_j) + (1-d)/N, damping factor d = 0.85, solved by power-iteration in matrix form PR = d*(W*)'PR + (1-d)(E*E'/n)

Optimized PageRank v1 (team original): adds a VIRTUAL (N+1)th node representing all papers outside the 16-paper set; reference weights become r_ij = 1/C where C = total references of p_i, and leftover weight r_i(N+1) = 1 - sum_j r_ij goes to the virtual node — breaks the small-network size limitation

Optimized PageRank v2 (team original): replaces the virtual node's uniform out-weights with Times-Cited-proportional weights r_(N+1)j = T_j / sum T_j, injecting real-world external citation impact into a tiny internal network

Network-classification analysis: degree distribution (power-law check), average path length L_G, Watts-Strogatz clustering coefficient CC, compared against a generated random network of equal size/average degree to diagnose small-world + scale-free structure
key_techniques: Gap-driven model selection: explicitly diagnose what each conventional measure (degree, closeness, betweenness, raw Borgatti DF) ignores — weight, cohesion-optimality, node ORDER — then build a single measure that closes all three gaps at once

Using the Shapley value's order-averaging property to fix the 'order of nodes ignored' defect of simple with/without-node DF subtraction — a genuinely clever reframing of influence as a cooperative-game payoff

Sign-interpretation trick: because smaller DF = more cohesive, a NEGATIVE Shapley value signals a positive contribution to cohesion; the more negative, the more influential — turns an abstract number into a clean ranking rule

Validation by cross-measure agreement: every Shapley ranking is placed side-by-side with the betweenness ranking (Table 3, Table 4); overlap is treated as corroboration and the few disagreements are explained mechanistically (e.g. Gwyneth Paltrow vs Kate Winslet — removal-impact vs bridging)
Virtual-node modeling to escape the 'tiny network makes PageRank meaningless' problem — a concrete, transferable engineering hack for applying web-scale algorithms to small bibliometric data

Progressive model refinement presented as a ladder (basic -> +virtual node -> +Times Cited), with a single results table (Table 5) showing how the top-5 ranking shifts at each step and WHY each paper moves

Two-front sensitivity analysis matched to each model's real risk: Shapley vs sample size k (the thing that can break it), PageRank vs damping factor d (the standard concern) — and honestly reporting that Shapley ranks ARE unstable below k~400

Second, self-sourced dataset (20 movie stars connected to Jude Law, weighted by number of co-appearances) to prove the method generalizes beyond the supplied Erdős data and to exercise the WEIGHTED-network path the Erdős set could not
why_it_won: "From a judge's view this paper wins on three axes. (1) Conceptual originality with rigor: instead of reaching for degree/betweenness like most teams, it fused game theory (Shapley) with a published cohesion measure and derived its own weighted-distance extension — a measure that demonstrably dominates the conventional ones on stated criteria (weight + cohesion + order). That is exactly the 'novel but defensible' sweet spot MCM rewards. (2) It covered BOTH network regimes the problem demands — undirected (Shapley) and directed (PageRank) — and didn't hand-wave the directed case; it built two original PageRank optimizations (virtual node, Times-Cited weighting) specifically engineered to fix PageRank's failure on a 16-node citation graph. (3) Intellectual honesty: the sensitivity analysis openly admits the Shapley ranks are unstable for small k and gives an operational threshold (k>400), while showing PageRank ranks are robust to d — judges trust teams that surface their own weaknesses. The dual-dataset validation (Erdős + movie stars) and the consistent cross-checking against betweenness show a team that tests its tools rather than just asserting them."
section_highlights: "Structure is textbook-strong and easy for a judge to navigate. The Summary Sheet states the thesis crisply (conventional measures miss weight/structure/order) and previews the whole method chain. A single 'Model Design Process' flowchart (Figure 1) splits the entire paper into an undirected branch (Cohesion + Shapley -> Shapley value) and a directed branch (basic -> optimized PageRank) — the reader knows the map before the details. Each model gets a parallel, repeated sub-structure: Assumptions -> Formulation (numbered equations) -> Application -> Strengths -> Weaknesses -> Utility, which makes the long paper scannable and signals self-awareness. Results are always tabular and comparative (Table 3/4 Shapley vs betweenness; Table 5 three PageRank variants side by side) so the payload is visible at a glance. The 'Utility' subsections translate each abstract measure into individual/organizational/national real-world uses (improve social influence, choose business collaborators, control interbank systemic risk), which gives the math an applied, ICM-flavored payoff. Equations are numbered and referenced; visualizations (PAJEK network, degree-distribution comparison vs a random net, extensive citation graph with the virtual node drawn in) directly support claims rather than decorate."
pitfalls_or_limitations: Weighted-cohesion definition is ad hoc and under-justified: d_ij = 1 + 1/p_ij is asserted, not derived, and the boundary cases are muddled — the text says for an UNWEIGHTED network p_ij is 'infinite' (Eq 7), which would make 1/p_ij = 0 and d_ij = 1 for every edge, collapsing all weighted distinctions; the reasoning here is sloppy and a sharp judge would poke it

Shapley ranking is demonstrably unstable: the team's own Figure 6 shows ranks of the same 5 co-authors swinging wildly (e.g. one author's rank bouncing 20->45->55) until k exceeds ~400 — the headline 'most influential = ALON, NOGA M.' rests on a noisy estimator, and they don't report confidence intervals or repeat-run variance

PageRank direction/data conventions are shaky: Eq 11 has an apparent typo (PR(p_i) in the numerator instead of PR(p_j)), and the citation-direction semantics flip between sections (r_ij = 1 means 'p_i cites p_j' in one place but the influence flow assumes citers point INTO the cited paper) — the matrix setup is hard to fully verify from the text

Times Cited values (e.g. 21688 for paper 14, 13250, 10616) are pulled from an external source that is left blank/unnamed in the equations and references ('Times Cited data of p_j from ___'), so a key input driving the final ranking is not reproducible

Erdős network is forced to be UNWEIGHTED by assumption (joint-publication counts assumed equal, Erdős-number-2 authors dropped 'to avoid too much work'), so the flagship dataset never exercises the weighted machinery the team built — the weighted path is only demonstrated on the small, self-selected movie-star set

The movie-star network has obvious OCR/diagram corruption (node labels 'Malf Damoni', 'Mikf Damoni', 'Matthew Damoni' all meant to be Matt Damon) and the 'cooperated with Jude Law' framing is loosely sourced; the dataset is illustrative rather than rigorously assembled

Claim that PageRank is 'not sensitive to d' is overstated as 'd has nothing to do with the Pagerank value' — d cannot change the RANK here only because the gaps between the top-5 scores are large; with closer scores d would matter, so the conclusion is dataset-specific, not general
References are present but mangled (journal names/volumes stripped, two raw baike.baidu.com URLs as sources), which slightly undercuts the scholarly polish
reusable_patterns: The 'enumerate-the-defects then build-one-measure-that-fixes-all' template: list what each baseline metric ignores (weight, order, cohesion-optimality), then engineer a single composite measure and argue dominance criterion-by-criterion. Directly reusable for any 'rank/score the most important X' problem.

Shapley-value-as-influence: when a problem asks 'whose removal/contribution matters most to a system,' model the system payoff with a characteristic function v and compute each player's average marginal contribution; approximate with random-permutation sampling when N! is infeasible. Transfers to networks, teams, portfolios, supply chains.

Virtual-node augmentation to make a global algorithm work on a small/closed dataset: add one node absorbing all outside mass, with leftover weight = 1 - sum(internal weights). A general fix whenever a contest gives you a tiny subgraph of a much larger real system.

Progressive-refinement results ladder: present model_v0 -> model_v1 -> model_v2 in ONE comparison table and narrate exactly which entries move and why at each refinement. Makes added complexity legible and earns credit for each step.

Cross-measure corroboration as validation: run your novel measure AND a standard one, tabulate both rankings, treat overlap as evidence and explain each disagreement mechanistically. Cheap, persuasive validation when ground truth is unavailable.

Risk-targeted sensitivity analysis: don't sweep a generic parameter — sweep the ONE parameter that could invalidate each model (sample size for a Monte Carlo estimator; damping factor for PageRank) and report an operational safe threshold (e.g. k>400).

Second self-collected dataset to (a) prove generalization and (b) exercise a code path the supplied data can't reach (here: the weighted-network branch via the movie-star network). Strong move when the official data is degenerate in some dimension.

Per-model 'Utility' ladder (individual / organizational / national level) to convert abstract math into ICM-style real-world impact — a clean rhetorical scaffold for the applied-significance section every ICM paper needs.
